title: SpaceX and Other Mega IPOs May Wait Years to Join the S&P 500
title_cn: SpaceX 和其他大型 IPO 可能要等待数年才能加入标准普尔 500 指数
summary: Mega-IPO candidates including SpaceX are expected to face a long road to entry to the S&P 500 Index, after the company that makes the rules rejected a proposal that included relaxing the requirement that they be profitable.

title: Becerra Advances for California Governor as Hilton Fights Steyer
title_cn: 希尔顿与斯泰尔较量，贝塞拉竞选加州州长
summary: Democratic former cabinet secretary Xavier Becerra is on track to advance in California’s governor’s race, setting up a November matchup that will hinge on whether Republican Steve Hilton or billionaire Tom Steyer claims the other run-off spot.

title: War in Iran Approaches 100 Day Mark
title_cn: 伊朗战争临近 100 天
summary: American forces headed off Iranian missile and drone attacks in the Persian Gulf as the war that began nearly 100 days ago continued to simmer with no resolution in the offing. Six ballistic missiles fired at Bahrain and Kuwait “were intercepted and a seventh did not reach its intended target,” US Central Command said in a statement on X late Friday. Bloomberg News Congressional Reporter Steven Dennis joined David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Pointed News Quiz | AI, Securities Fraud, Movies
title_cn: 针对性新闻测验 |人工智能、证券欺诈、电影
summary: David Gura, Christina Ruffini, and Lisa Mateo of “Bloomberg This Weekend” play Pointed! Wager your points, leverage your bets and answer wisely. A new quiz is available to play each week on Bloomberg.com (Source: Bloomberg)

title: FIFA Prepares for World Cup 2026
title_cn: 国际足联为 2026 年世界杯做准备
summary: FIFA is offering assurances to New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani that fans may bring water into World Cup stadiums — within certain rules. Mamdani raised concerns Thursday about reports that FIFA had banned fans from bringing bottles of water to World Cup matches, demanding answers from FIFA. Bloomberg Opinion Business of Sports Columnist Adam Minter joined David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Frasers Weighs £500 Million Bid for Shopping Center, Sky Reports
title_cn: 据天空报道，星狮集团考虑出价 5 亿英镑收购购物中心
summary: Mike Ashley’s Frasers Group Plc is considering a £500 million ($667 million) bid for Metrocentre, one of the UK’s biggest shopping centers, Sky News reports.

title: Trump Holds Profanity-Filled Call with Netanyahu
title_cn: 特朗普与内塔尼亚胡进行充满脏话的通话
summary: President Donald Trump has spent months projecting confidence that a deal to end the Iran conflict is within reach. Israel’s determination to press ahead with its own objectives underscores his tenuous control over the outcome of the crisis. Former US ambassador to Israel under Obama, Tom Nides, joined David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: How Bolivia's Plans to Tap Mineral Riches Are Being Tested by Mass Unrest
title_cn: 玻利维亚开采矿产资源的计划如何受到大规模骚乱的考验
summary: Bolivia’s new president, Rodrigo Paz, is attempting to rebrand the country as a destination for foreign investment after two decades of socialist rule. Sitting atop some of the world’s largest lithium resources and vast mineral reserves, Bolivia has long struggled to translate its natural resources into economic prosperity. Paz argues that clearer laws, stability, and closer ties with international investors can unlock growth. But, just six months into President Paz’s term, his hopes are meeting the reality of unrest at home and violent protests.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Broadway Shows Eye London, NY Wooing With Tax Credits
title_cn: 百老汇关注纽约伦敦，寻求税收抵免
summary:
Catching a Broadway musical is a quintessential experience for many in New York City. But it’s not cheap, not for showgoers nor for show producers, who saw costs go up in the aftermath of the pandemic. Producers have been considering taking their next big hits somewhere else — to Atlanta, Chicago and even overseas to London, where it can be significantly cheaper to put on a good show.

Bloomberg Tax, State Tax Policy Journalist Danielle Muoio Dunn joined David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Proposes New Tariffs Over Forced Labor
title_cn: 美国提议对强迫劳动征收新关税
summary: The Trump administration has hit on a new way to impose tariffs on 60 nations that supply almost all US imports — accuse them of failing to enforce bans on foreign goods made with forced labor. Bloomberg News Global Trade Editor Brendan Murray joined David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Macro Soothsayers, Stock Analysts See Vastly Different Markets
title_cn: 宏观预言家、股票分析师看到的市场截然不同
summary: The strongest earnings season in years is coming to a close, and the Wall Street analysts who focus on individual companies have rarely been this bullish.

title: Raizen Inks $13 Billion Out-of-Court Deal With Creditors
title_cn: Raizen 与债权人达成 130 亿美元的庭外协议
summary: Raizen SA reached an out-of-court restructuring agreement with the majority of its creditors, according to a regulatory filing, marking a key step in the Brazilian sugar-and-ethanol producer’s efforts to rework its debt.

title: Expensive Knicks Seats, Binge Drinking, Cake in a Cup  | BTW: Headlines You Should See
title_cn: 尼克斯队的昂贵座位、酗酒、杯中蛋糕|顺便说一句：你应该看到的头条新闻
summary: Catch up on all the headlines with BTW. Lisa Mateo, Christina Ruffini and David Gura dive into the headlines you may have missed on Bloomberg This Weekend. Watch the show LIVE every Saturday and Sunday morning: (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Sanctions Cuba’s Díaz-Canel as Trump Ramps Up Pressure
title_cn: 特朗普加大压力，美国制裁古巴迪亚斯-卡内尔
summary: The US slapped sanctions on Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel and members of his family, further ratcheting up pressure on the communist-run Caribbean island. CNN, Havana Bureau Chief Patrick Oppmann joined David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Asia-to-US Container Rates Spike 109% Since Iran War Started
title_cn: 自伊朗战争爆发以来，亚洲至美国的集装箱运价飙升 109%
summary: Container shipping rates jumped over the past week amid higher fuel costs, congestion at some Asian ports and a pickup in demand heading into a peak season for booking ocean freight.

title: Is the Dollar Losing Its Luster?
title_cn: 美元正在失去光彩吗？
summary: Economist Ken Rogoff argues that the dollar’s position as the world’s dominant currency is unlikely to disappear overnight, but faces mounting pressures. On the international stage, the war in Iran, rising military spending, and China’s efforts to expand use of the yuan build on a trend toward a more multipolar financial system. At home, persistent US deficits, rising debt burdens, and higher interest rates darken America’s fiscal trajectory. Rogoff says the dollar remains deeply embedded in global trade and finance, but warns that the gradual erosion of its dominance is already underway.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Publishers Adapt to AI, Space Race Centers on Satellites
title_cn: 出版商适应人工智能，卫星太空竞赛中心
summary: People Inc. finds ways around the disruption.

title: Paramount Open to Selling Kids Channels to Quell EU Fears Over $110 Billion Warner Deal
title_cn: 派拉蒙愿意出售儿童频道以平息欧盟对华纳 1100 亿美元交易的担忧
summary: Paramount Skydance Corp. is prepared — if necessary — to divest some children’s TV network assets to help win European Union approval of its $110 billion bid for Warner Bros. Discovery Inc.

title: Indonesia’s Crisis of Confidence as Markets Decode Prabowo
title_cn: 市场解读普拉博沃，印尼面临信心危机
summary: Indonesia spent much of this week confronting a question that might have seemed unthinkable a few short years ago: what happens when investors stop believing in Southeast Asia’s largest economy?

title: Chris Davis on His Biggest Investing Mistakes
title_cn: 克里斯·戴维斯谈他最大的投资错误
summary:
Barry sits down with Chris Davis,  Chairman and Portfolio Manager at Davis Funds. They discuss his approach to managing risk and the key elements changing the economy. Chris and Barry also discuss Chris's mentors including Charlie Munger, and how he settled into the family business. 
 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Charting the Global Economy: Jobs, Inflation Feed Rate-Hike Bets
title_cn: 绘制全球经济图：就业、通胀加息押注
summary: A blowout US jobs report prompted bets that the Federal Reserve will raise interest rates in 2026 as the ongoing Iran war fans inflation risk.

title: China’s Regulator to Deepen Focus on Returns in Fund Overhaul
title_cn: 中国监管机构将深化对基金改革回报的关注
summary: The securities regulator in China signaled that the fund management industry needs to accelerate its shift toward delivering sustainable long-term returns.

title: Why Susquehanna Is Building a Prediction Market Business | Odd Lots
title_cn: 为什么萨斯奎哈纳要建立预测市场业务？零股
summary: Prediction markets that enable you to bet on pretty much everything are everywhere nowadays. But there's still a big question over whether they can expand to include larger institutional investors like hedge funds. Part of the problem is that a lot of prediction market contracts are illiquid and trading volumes can sometimes be shallow. That's where trading firm Susquehanna International Group comes in. In this episode, recorded live at New York's City Winery, we talk to Jeremy Maletz, Susquehanna's head of macro trading and prediction markets, about the firm's market-making business with Kalshi. We talk about how big investors could use prediction markets, what Susquehanna is seeing in terms of flows, how a market-maker hedges risk on these contracts, and how it makes money from them. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Why Susquehanna Is Building a Prediction Markets Business
title_cn: 萨斯奎哈纳为何要建立预测市场业务
summary: Creating an asset class for bets on anything.

title: Odd Lots: Why Susquehanna Builds Prediction Markets (Podcast)
title_cn: 奇怪的事情：为什么萨斯奎哈纳建立预测市场（播客）
summary: Prediction markets that enable you to bet on pretty much everything are everywhere nowadays. But there’s still a big question over whether they can expand to include larger institutional investors like hedge funds. Part of the problem is that a lot of prediction market contracts are illiquid and trading volumes can sometimes be shallow. That’s where trading firm Susquehanna International Group comes in. In this episode, recorded live at New York’s City Winery, we talk to Jeremy Maletz, Susquehan

title: RBNZ’s Breman Says Country Finding Solutions to Protectionism
title_cn: 新西兰联储布雷曼表示，国家正在寻找保护主义的解决方案
summary: New Zealand’s central bank governor, Anna Breman, is seeing encouraging signs that will help counter the negative effects of deepening protectionism and a decline in global cooperation.

title: Austria Downgrade Ends Era in Club of Europe’s Safest Borrowers
title_cn: 奥地利降级结束欧洲最安全借款人俱乐部时代
summary: Austria lost its last top credit score from a major assessor because of persistently high budget deficits, ending a decades-long streak as one of Europe’s best-rated issuers.

title: Indonesian Officials Vow Again to Steady Rupiah, Draw Funds
title_cn: 印度尼西亚官员再次誓言稳定卢比并筹集资金
summary: Indonesia’s finance and central bank officials said they will step up efforts to stabilize the currency and attract inflows after a week where the nation’s stocks tumbled at the fastest pace worldwide and its currency sank to all-time lows.

title: Philippine Court Drops Remaining Marcos Ill-Gotten Wealth Case
title_cn: 菲律宾法院撤销马科斯不当财富案的剩余案件
summary: The Philippine anti-graft court dismissed the remaining claims in a civil case seeking to recover assets allegedly acquired by the late former President Ferdinand Marcos and his family.

title: Nigerian Startup Bets Big on Drones
title_cn: 尼日利亚初创公司在无人机上投入巨资
summary: As drone attacks become increasingly common across the Sahel, Nigerian startup Terra Industries is expanding into Ghana and scaling production of drone and counter-drone systems. Backed by $34 million from investors including Palantir co-founder Joe Lonsdale and Lux Capital, the company aims to produce tens of thousands of systems over the coming years as governments across the region bolster their defenses. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: China, New Zealand Hold Trade Talks to Deepen Cooperation
title_cn: 中国与新西兰举行贸易谈判深化合作
summary: China and New Zealand held a bilateral trade commission meeting in Beijing on Friday, where officials exchanged views on deepening economic ties and cooperation in regional and multilateral frameworks.

title: HKMA Says It’s Implemented New Bank Rules for Opening Accounts
title_cn: 金管局表示已实施新的银行开户规则
summary: Hong Kong has implemented guidelines for banks similar to the new regulatory requirements for brokers on managing mainland Chinese customers’ accounts, the Hong Kong Monetary Authority said.

title: Bitcoin Drop Tests Retail Investor Strength
title_cn: 比特币下跌考验散户投资者实力
summary: For years, Wall Street has benefited from one of the most reliable forces in modern markets: a retail-trader army willing to buy almost anything. Friday offered a glimpse of what happens when several of those trades come under pressure at the same time. Artificial-intelligence stocks suffered their sharpest selloff in months, Bitcoin fell below $60,000 and bond yields surged as traders revived bets that the Federal Reserve’s next move could be a rate increase. Bloomberg Intelligence Senior Commodity Strategist Mike McGlone and Navy Federal Credit Union Chief Economist Heather Long joined David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Elon Musk to Attend ASML’s Private Tech Event to Discuss Terafab
title_cn: 埃隆·马斯克将出席 ASML 的私人技术活动讨论 Terafab
summary: Elon Musk will virtually attend a closed door technology conference run by ASML Holding NV to discuss his Terafab project, which the chip-equipment maker considers a “serious endeavor.”

title: Grindr Growing From Just Dating App to Politics, AI
title_cn: Grindr 从单纯的约会应用程序发展到政治、人工智能
summary: Grindr Inc. unveiled a handful of new app features and shared more details around its premium subscription tier as part of a larger effort to integrate artificial intelligence across its LGBTQ dating platform. The premium plan unlocks access to new AI features and deeper personalization, Grindr said Monday in a statement. The company is also investing further in AI-powered safety tools and in behind-the-scenes operations, Chief Executive Officer George Arison said in an interview with Bloomberg This Weekend. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Trump Signals Interest in US Owning Stakes in Top AI Labs
title_cn: 特朗普表示有兴趣在美国顶级人工智能实验室中持有股份
summary: President Donald Trump expressed interest in the US government holding equity stakes in leading artificial intelligence developers, saying that he planned to discuss the idea of a partnership with AI companies’ executives as soon as next week. Bloomberg Surveillance Co-Host and Chief Political Correspondent Annmarie Horden joined David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Jensen Huang Commands Seoul Stage With Night of Street Theatre
title_cn: 黄仁勋以街头剧场之夜指挥首尔舞台
summary: For one Friday night, Hongdae wasn’t Seoul’s trendiest neighborhood. It was Jensen Huang’s stage.

title: Wall Street Week | AI-era Internet, Spaceport Investment, Bolivia’s Investability
title_cn: 华尔街周刊 | AI时代互联网、航天港投资、玻利维亚的可投资性
summary: This week, the dollar remains the world’s dominant currency, but economist Ken Rogoff thinks rising debt and geopolitical shifts are eroding its position as the world’s reserve currency. And, AI-powered search is reducing web traffic, forcing publishers to rethink how they attract audiences and generate revenue online. Will competition between the US and China to launch satellites create the next major infrastructure boom in the commercial space industry? Bolivia wants to benefit from foreign capital flows into Latin America and harness its mineral reserves, but political instability remains a significant challenge.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Discussion on the Iconoclast Dinner Experience and Its Cultural Impact
title_cn: 讨论反传统晚餐体验及其文化影响
summary: Esi Eggleston Bracey, former Unilever Global Chief Growth & Marketing Officer, shared her involvement with the Iconoclast dinner experience, highlighting its significance as a coveted event in the culinary world. She described how the event, founded by Dr. Leslie, has been developed over eleven years to identify and bring together top influencers and power players in culinary cuisine. She speaks with Romaine Bostick & Katie Greifeld on "The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Market Sell-Off Analyzed Amid NASDAQ 100 Decline and Rising Yields
title_cn: 纳斯达克 100 指数下跌和收益率上升期间的市场抛售分析
summary: Jeremy Siegel, Professor Emeritus of Finance at the Wharton School, discussed the recent market sell-off during a live financial news segment. The conversation focused on the sharp decline in the NASDAQ 100, which was described as an AI-led rout coinciding with climbing yields. He speaks with Romaine Bostick & Katie Greifeld on "The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: The $200 Billion Data Center Transforming Louisiana
title_cn: 耗资 2000 亿美元的数据中心正在改变路易斯安那州
summary: Meta's Mark Zuckerberg wanted to build a massive data center. And Louisiana leaders wanted Meta to choose their state as its home. But making that happen took a lot of negotiation — and secrecy. Bloomberg reporter Riley Griffin takes host Sarah Holder inside the deal. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Marvell Technology, Flex to Join S&P 500 Later This Month
title_cn: Marvell Technology 和 Flex 将于本月晚些时候加入标准普尔 500 指数
summary: Marvell Technology Inc. and Flex Ltd. will join the S&P 500 in the latest quarterly rebalance, S&P Dow Jones Indices said Friday.

title: DocuSign Shares Drop After Full-Year Guidance Disappoints Investors
title_cn: 全年指引令投资者失望后 DocuSign 股价下跌
summary: DocuSign's shares declined amid a broadly negative market day following the company's full-year guidance, which failed to excite investors. Allan Thygesen, CEO of DocuSign, discussed the company's AI-powered intelligent agreement management platform, highlighting strong adoption with 40,000 customers currently live on the platform. He speaks with Romaine Bostick and Katie Greifeld on "The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Jobs Report Fuels Fed-Rate Hike Bets | The Close 6/5/2026
title_cn: 就业报告助长美联储加息押注收盘价 6/5/2026
summary: Bloomberg Television brings you the latest news and analysis leading up to the final minutes and seconds before and after the closing bell on Wall Street. Today's guests are Edward Jones Head of Investment Strategy Mona Mahajan, Docusign CEO Allan Thygesen, Guggenheim Partners Senior Analyst of Retail & Commerce Simeon Siegel, Sperling Economics President Gene Sperling, Interactive Brokers Chief Strategist Steve Sosnick, Wharton School Professor Emeritus of Finance Jeremy Siegel, KPMG Chief Economist Diane Swank, DVx Ventures Co-Founder & Partner Karim Bousta, & Former Unilever Global Chief Growth & Marketing Officer Esi Eggleston Bracey. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: SpaceX Inks $30 Billion Computing Power Deal With Google
title_cn: SpaceX 与 Google 签署价值 300 亿美元的计算能力协议
summary: Bloomberg's Bailey Lipschultz breaks down a busy week in SpaceX news, as the aerospace company prepares for an IPO. SpaceX and Google entered into an agreement for cloud services where Google has agreed to pay SpaceX $920 million per month for computing, something Lipschultz says is indicative of the expansion of the scope of work SpaceX is looking to do.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Apollo Wraps Up $35 Billion Debt to Buy AI Chips for Anthropic
title_cn: Apollo 偿还 350 亿美元债务，为 Anthropic 购买人工智能芯片
summary: Apollo Global Management Inc. and Blackstone Inc. have finalized a $35 billion financing package for Anthropic PBC to expand its AI infrastructure, marking the latest mega-deal in the artificial intelligence race.

title: Google-Tied Prepaid Energy Bonds See Flood of Muni Trader Demand
title_cn: 与谷歌挂钩的预付能源债券迎来了市政交易者的大量需求
summary: Google parent Alphabet Inc.’s municipal-bond market debut was met by a surge of investor interest.

title: BOE’s Bailey Warns of Possible AI Rationing on Capacity Limits
title_cn: 京东方贝利警告人工智能可能会限制产能
summary: Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey suggested that artificial intelligence may need to be rationed because a lack of energy capacity will restrain the ability for every economic sector to deploy the new technology.

title: SpaceX Inks $30 Billion Computing Power Deal With Google
title_cn: SpaceX 与 Google 签署价值 300 亿美元的计算能力协议
summary: Alphabet Inc.’s Google has agreed to pay Elon Musk’s SpaceX $920 million a month for computing power as part of a cloud services deal that runs through mid-2029, its second such agreement with an AI competitor in a matter of weeks.

title: Tech Leaders Mull the Next Phase of AI as Top Labs Plan IPOs
title_cn: 顶级实验室计划首次公开募股，技术领导者正在考虑人工智能的下一阶段
summary: Executives and academics discuss the new unknowns emerging for AI

title: Bloomberg Money 6/5/2026
title_cn: 彭博财经 2026 年 6 月 5 日
summary: Bloomberg Money takes the pulse of your financial life, powered by the reporting of our global newsroom. Today's guests: Charles Schwab Chief Investment Strategist Liz Ann Sonders and JPMorgan Asset Management Chief Market Strategist for Americas Gabriela Santos.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Meta Considers Raising Billions in Share Sale, FT Reports
title_cn: 据《金融时报》报道，Meta 考虑通过出售股票筹集数十亿美元
summary: Meta Platforms Inc. is considering raising equity after Alphabet Inc.’s successful $85 billion sale, according to the Financial Times. Meta shares fell on the report.

title: Rubrik CEO's Big AI Warning
title_cn: Rubrik 首席执行官的大人工智能警告
summary: Rubrik CEO Bipul Sinha explains why AI is transforming cybersecurity, warns that AI agents bring "100x more risk," and discusses how businesses must shift from preventing attacks to rapidly recovering from them. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: S&P’s SpaceX Snub Shows Elon Musk the Power of Index Gatekeepers
title_cn: 标准普尔对 SpaceX 的冷遇向埃隆·马斯克展示了指数看门人的力量
summary: Elon Musk once said passive investing has “gone too far,” mocked environmental, social and governance indexes as a “scam” and endured a long wait to get Tesla Inc. into the S&P 500.

title: Tech Trade Under Pressure; Musk Sells His Vision of the Future | Bloomberg Tech 6/05/2026
title_cn: 科技贸易面临压力；马斯克兜售他的未来愿景彭博科技 2026 年 6 月 5 日
summary: Bloomberg’s Ed Ludlow breaks down Elon Musk's vision of the future and how he enchanted investors even as he brushed aside questions about the SpaceX IPO at an investor event. Plus, he takes a look at May's hot jobs report even as tech stocks are under pressure with investors reassessing the path for interest rates. And, he recaps conversations with three of the biggest names in AI at the Bloomberg Tech event in San Francisco. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Revolut Weighs Secondary Share Sale at $115 Billion Valuation
title_cn: Revolut 考虑以 1150 亿美元估值进行二次出售
summary: Revolut Ltd. is looking to run a secondary share sale that would value the digital bank at $115 billion, on the heels of receiving a UK bank license and applying for a charter in the US, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

title: Index Ventures' Achadjian on Backing Ex-SpaceX Workers
title_cn: Index Ventures 的 Achadjian 支持前 SpaceX 员工
summary: Software has dominated venture capital for years, but as AI moves beyond the digital world and into manufacturing, robotics, and industrial systems, investors are betting the next trillion-dollar opportunities wraps software into the physical world. Nina Achadjian, partner at Index Ventures, an early backer of companies including Anthropic, Physical Intelligence, and ServiceTitan, discusses the next frontier in AI and the SpaceX IPO's significance on the venture space. She joins Ed Ludlow on "Bloomberg Tech" (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Apex Is the 'Ford' of Satellites, Says CEO
title_cn: 首席执行官表示，Apex 是卫星界的“福特”
summary: Ian Cinnamon, Apex CEO, joins after the company raised more than $200 million as it works to increase production of satellite platforms for commercial firms and government work, including the development of President Trump's Golden Dome missile defense shield. He joins Ed Ludlow on "Bloomberg Tech." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Orbital Data Centers Face Space-Based Challenges
title_cn: 轨道数据中心面临天基挑战
summary: Philip Johnston, Starcloud CEO, joins to discuss the challenges of building and maintaining orbital data centers in space, this as SpaceX says it wants to ultimately deploy 100 gigawatts of AI compute capacity in orbit. He joins Ed Ludlow on "Bloomberg Tech." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: AI Not Holding Back Companies From Hiring: Yale Budget Lab
title_cn: 人工智能不会阻碍公司招聘：耶鲁预算实验室
summary: The May jobs report came in stronger than expected, but tech stocks are under pressure as investors reassess the path for interest rates. Martha Gimbel, executive director of the Yale Budget Lab, sees no major AI impact in economic data. She joins Ed Ludlow on "Bloomberg Tech." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: China Escalates Patrols Near Taiwan Over Japan-Philippines Talks
title_cn: 中国因日本与菲律宾会谈而加强在台湾附近的巡逻
summary: China said it launched a special maritime operation east of Taiwan, ramping up its response to a recent agreement between Japan and the Philippines to negotiate maritime borders in the area and deepen defense cooperation.

title: Trump Speaks on Air Force One to Wisconsin
title_cn: 特朗普乘坐空军一号前往威斯康星州发表讲话
summary:
President Trump announced that top artificial intelligence leaders will visit the White House next week to discuss how the technology can benefit Americans.

Speaking to reporters aboard Air Force One en route to Wisconsin on Friday, the President shared plans for the upcoming meeting following questions from Bloomberg’s Annmarie Hordern. During the press pool exchange, Hordern also questioned Trump regarding Senator Bernie Sanders and the rising costs of basketball game tickets (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Plans to Tap Bolivia’s Vast Mineral Riches Hit by Mass Unrest
title_cn: 计划开采玻利维亚因大规模骚乱而蕴藏的丰富矿产资源
summary: Mass unrest across Bolivia is stalling the new government’s plans to tap the nation’s vast mineral wealth using foreign capital.

title: Trump Speaks on Sanders Economic Policies
title_cn: 特朗普谈桑德斯经济政策
summary: Speaking to reporters aboard Air Force One en route to Wisconsin on Friday, President Trump spoke on Senator Bernie Sanders and their similarities on economic policies. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Trump on Rising Cost of Basketball Games Tickets
title_cn: 特朗普谈篮球比赛门票价格上涨
summary: Speaking to reporters aboard Air Force One en route to Wisconsin on Friday, President Trump responds to concerns over the rising costs of basketball game tickets. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Trump Announces AI Leaders White House Visit
title_cn: 特朗普宣布人工智能领导人访问白宫
summary:
Speaking to reporters aboard Air Force One en route to Wisconsin on Friday, President Trump announced that top artificial intelligence leaders will visit the White House next week to discuss how the technology can benefit Americans.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Merz Warns of Far-Right ‘Big Bang’ in Eastern German Elections
title_cn: 默茨警告东德选举中极右翼“大爆炸”
summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz warned of a major shakeup in two key eastern German state elections in September where the Alternative for Germany leads in the polls, urging the political center to work together to counter the far-right party.

title: Ukraine Targets St. Petersburg After Putin Refuses Talks
title_cn: 普京拒绝会谈后乌克兰瞄准圣彼得堡
summary: Ukraine launched a drone barrage at St. Petersburg on Saturday, the final day of a major investment forum, hours after President Vladimir Putin spoke there and refused a proposal for talks with Kyiv.

title: Lithuanian Coalition Collapses After Clash Over Defense Plans
title_cn: 立陶宛联军因防御计划发生冲突而崩溃
summary: Lithuania’s Social Democrats have moved to dissolve their ruling coalition with junior partner Dawn of Nemunas following a clash over defense policy.

title: The Albanian Island at the Center of Anger Over Kushner’s Resort Plans
title_cn: 阿尔巴尼亚岛成为库什纳度假村计划愤怒的中心
summary: The ride from the Albanian mainland to the island of Sazan takes just 20 minutes in a speedboat.

title: US Intercepts Fresh Iranian Attacks as Peace Talks Stall
title_cn: 和平谈判陷入僵局，美国拦截伊朗新一轮袭击
summary: US and Iranian forces traded fresh attacks overnight, at the end of a week that saw the worst flare-up in tensions since the shaky ceasefire started and little progress toward an interim peace deal.

title: Xi’s Confidant Cai Expands Influence With Party School Head Post
title_cn: 习近平心腹蔡出任党校校长扩大影响力
summary: China’s fifth-most senior leader has taken over leadership of the Communist Party’s top training academy, marking the latest expansion of responsibilities for one of President Xi Jinping’s trusted aides.

title: ‘What Will Happen to Us?’ Southern Lebanese Christians Ask
title_cn: 黎巴嫩南部基督徒问道：“我们会发生什么？”
summary: Rita Elias and her family fled southern Lebanon to shelter with relatives in Beirut during the last war between Hezbollah and Israel in 2024, returning only after a truce. This time, she stayed to protect her property, her husband’s trucking business and their tobacco farm.

title: Iceland Debates Benefits of Euro Entry as Vote on EU Talks Nears
title_cn: 随着欧盟谈判投票临近，冰岛讨论加入欧元区的好处
summary: Iceland’s upcoming referendum on talks with the European Union has also sparked a debate over whether the country should give up its currency.

title: Swiss Army’s Push to Keep Soldiers Is Going to the Ballot Box
title_cn: 瑞士军队保留士兵的努力即将进入投票箱
summary: Switzerland’s struggle to keep its military staffed at a time of global geopolitical tensions is about to play out with a national vote on the terms of conscription.

title: Burnham Looks Toward Starmer Showdown After Reform UK Stumbles
title_cn: 英国改革遭遇挫折后，伯纳姆期待斯塔默摊牌
summary: Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham was indignant when a broadcaster challenged him this week over his refusal to spell out wider ambitions for higher office beyond winning the special election he’s fighting.

title: Thailand May Seek Thaksin Bankruptcy Over $538 Million Tax Debt
title_cn: 泰国可能因5.38亿美元税收债务寻求他信破产
summary: Thailand’s Revenue Department said it may pursue bankruptcy proceedings against former Prime Minister Thaksin Shinawatra if it is unable to fully recover 17.6 billion baht ($538 million) in outstanding tax liabilities.

title: Anwar’s Battle Against Allies Shapes Up as Key Malaysian States Call Polls
title_cn: 马来西亚主要州举行民意调查，安瓦尔与盟友的斗争即将展开
summary: Malaysian Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim’s grip on power is set to be tested as he prepares to face off against his erstwhile allies, with his party’s urban base in play in two upcoming state elections.

title: US Adds 172,000 Jobs in May, Topping All Estimates | Balance of Power: Late Edition 6/5/2026
title_cn: 美国 5 月份新增就业岗位 172,000 个，超出所有预期权力平衡：最新版 2026 年 6 月 5 日
summary: "Balance of Power: Late Edition" focuses on the intersection of politics and global business. On today's show, Heather Boushey, Former White House Council of Economic Advisers Member, says the 172,000 jobs added in the month of May is good sign for the US economy, but warns that 'we're not out of the woods yet,' Neil Bradley, US Chamber of Commerce Executive VP, discusses the latest US jobs report and says while consumers are worried, 'they haven't pulled back on spending,' and Carl Skau, Acting Executive Director of the UN World Food Programme, says the world has seen a 'three-fold' increase in world hunger over the last five years and discusses the humanitarian crises in the Middle East. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US-Iran Standoff Drags On as Conflict Nears 100-Day Mark
title_cn: 冲突接近 100 天，美伊对峙持续
summary: The US and Iran remained at loggerheads over any potential truce heading into the weekend, with the conflict nearing the 100-day mark and Tehran saying that it and Oman have sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz.

title: WFP's Skau on Middle East Crisis and Global Hunger
title_cn: 世界粮食计划署斯考谈中东危机和全球饥饿
summary: Carl Skau, Acting Executive Director of the UN World Food Programme, says the world has seen a 'three-fold' increase in world hunger over the last five years and discusses the humanitarian crises in the Middle East as well as countries like Sudan and Sri Lanka. He speaks with Joe Mathieu and Kailey Leinz on Bloomberg's Balance of Power. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Why India’s Gen-Z ‘Cockroach’ Movement Is a Worry for Modi
title_cn: 为什么印度 Z 世代“蟑螂”运动令莫迪担忧
summary: What started out as a social media joke among Gen Z is turning into an irritant for the political establishment in India, as the “Cockroach Janta Party” draws support from young people frustrated by a lack of jobs and a string of controversies in the education system.

title: Bradley Talks Jobs, Government Stakes in AI Companies
title_cn: 布拉德利谈论就业和政府在人工智能公司中的股份
summary: Neil Bradley, US Chamber of Commerce Executive VP, discusses the latest US jobs report and says while consumers are worried, 'they haven't pulled back on spending yet.' He also spoke out against the government having stake in AI companies, saying it would 'break down the wall' between government and the private sector. Neil speaks with Joe Mathieu and Kailey Leinz on Bloomberg's Balance of Power. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Boushey Discusses Jobs Report, Wages and Inflation
title_cn: 布希讨论就业报告、工资和通货膨胀
summary: Heather Boushey, Former White House Council of Economic Advisers Member, says the 172,000 jobs added in the month of May is good sign for the US economy, but warns that 'we're not out of the woods yet.' She also discusses the effect of stagnant wages and rising prices on the US consumer and says 'families are really challenged.' Heather speaks with Joe Mathieu and Kailey Leinz on Bloomberg's Balance of Power. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Army Warns Lawmakers Draft Legislation Risks Data Center Push
title_cn: 美国陆军警告立法者草案立法风险推动数据中心发展
summary: The US Army has warned against lawmakers’ efforts to increase oversight for Defense Department data centers, saying the proposals risk jeopardizing efforts to build the facilities on military bases just as demand for computing power soars.

title: FIFA Will Let Fans Bring Bottled Water to World Cup Matches, With Some Caveats
title_cn: 国际足联将允许球迷携带瓶装水观看世界杯比赛，但有一些注意事项
summary: FIFA is offering assurances to New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani that fans may bring water into World Cup stadiums — within certain rules.

title: Trump Pledges ‘Good Things’ for Farmers Without Giving Details
title_cn: 特朗普承诺为农民提供“好事”，但没有透露细节
summary: Donald Trump said that farmers would see “good things” happen in the coming months, as the president sought to bolster his relationship an agriculture industry who have been battered by his tariffs as well as high fuel and fertilizer costs triggered by his war with Iran.

title: Tech Stocks Lead Selloff Amid Strong Jobs Report | Bloomberg Businessweek Daily 6/5/2026
title_cn: 就业报告强劲，科技股领跌彭博商业周刊日报 6/5/2026
summary: Today, Mike Mckee and Ira Jersey break down a strong May jobs report, as the US ads 172,000 jobs in the last month. Then, Goldman Sachs partner John Flood joins Natalia Kniazhevich to discuss the market outlook as tech stocks lead a major selloff. Plus, Dan Arnold, SVP of national operations at Protos Security, and Bloomberg's Vanessa Perdomo-Maglione talk security challenges of the upcoming World Cup and how US cities are preparing to deal with millions of fans flooding into town. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Trump Signs AI Memo Addressing Issues in Anthropic-Pentagon Feud
title_cn: 特朗普签署人工智能备忘录解决人类与五角大楼不和的问题
summary: US national security agencies must make a priority of working with more than one artificial intelligence provider, according to a memo from President Donald Trump that follows an extended feud between the Pentagon and Anthropic PBC, which until recently was the only vendor approved for classified military use.
